questions.explanation: Sewage is wastewater released by homes, industries, and other sources containing dissolved and suspended impurities.

questions.text: Impurities present in sewage are called:
questions.explanation: These include organic impurities, inorganic impurities, and microbes.
WWTP is where physical, chemical, and biological processes remove contaminants from wastewater.

questions.text: Which of these is used to disinfect water?
questions.explanation: Chemicals like chlorine and ozone are used to kill germs in water before it is distributed.

questions.text: The solids that settle at the bottom during sewage treatment are called:
questions.explanation: Sludge is removed by a scraper and transferred to a separate tank for decomposition.

questions.text: What is the function of bar screens in a treatment plant?
questions.explanation: Bar screens remove large items like rags, sticks, cans, and plastic packets.

questions.text: Bacteria that work in the absence of oxygen are called:
questions.explanation: Anaerobic bacteria decompose sludge to produce biogas.
Dried sludge returns organic matter and nutrients to the soil.

questions.text: Vermi-processing toilets use ________ to treat human waste.
questions.explanation: Earthworms convert human excreta into high-quality vermicompost.

questions.text: Which of the following should not be thrown down the drain?
questions.explanation: Solid wastes like tea leaves or food remains can choke the drains and prevent oxygen flow.
